scope.summary:
Bind the native Rust editor layer to the existing /knowledge/documents/* backend APIs. No backend changes are permitted; this MT is pure frontend wiring.

Goal: create a typed Rust client module at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/knowledge_documents.rs that exposes async functions for every route in the backend knowledge_documents API. The diff shape is one new file plus a re-export wiring it into the handshake-native crate. Reuse the existing WP-011 backend_client.rs module (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs) for the shared HTTP client and base-URL resolution; do NOT introduce a new bespoke config struct if backend_client.rs already provides it.

Exact files to create/edit:
  - CREATE: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/knowledge_documents.rs
  - CREATE: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/mod.rs  (new `backend` subdir module: `pub mod knowledge_documents;`)
  - EDIT:   src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs  (add `pub mod backend;` if not already present; reachable as handshake_native::backend::knowledge_documents)

The backend HTTP surface (read from handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs) exposes these routes, all under the base URL served by the running handshake_core server (obtained from the existing backend_client.rs BackendClient):
  POST   /knowledge/documents                          -> create_document
  POST   /knowledge/documents/import                   -> import_document
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id             -> load_document (returns document + tree + code_nodes)
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/draft       -> load_document_draft
  PUT    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/draft       -> upsert_document_draft
  DELETE /knowledge/documents/:document_id/draft       -> clear_document_draft
  PUT    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/save        -> save_document (optimistic-concurrency, expected_version field)
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/blocks      -> load_blocks
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/history     -> load_history (paginated: ?limit=&offset=)
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/history/:v  -> load_history_version
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/projection  -> export_projection (?format=markdown|html|plain_text|wiki_loom|context_bundle)
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/embeds      -> list_embeds
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/embeds/broken -> list_broken_embeds
  POST   /knowledge/documents/embeds/:embed_id/repair  -> repair_embed
  GET    /knowledge/documents/:document_id/backlinks   -> list_backlinks
  POST   /knowledge/documents/:document_id/backlinks   -> rebuild_backlinks
  POST   /knowledge/documents/:document_id/rename      -> rename_document
  POST   /knowledge/documents/:document_id/move        -> move_document
  POST   /knowledge/documents/batch                    -> batch_documents

Every request MUST include the mandatory identity headers:
  x-hsk-actor-id           (required)
  x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id (required)
  x-hsk-session-run-id     (required)
  x-hsk-actor-kind         (optional; omit for least-privileged read-only)
  x-hsk-correlation-id     (optional)
Missing required headers cause the backend to return 400. Provide a HskDocumentHeaders struct and a helper that populates them from editor session context.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Define Rust structs mirroring the backend request/response bodies (use serde::Deserialize/Serialize):
     - CreateDocumentRequest { workspace_id, title, content_json?, schema_version?, project_ref?, folder_ref? }
     - SaveDocumentRequest { expected_version: i64, content_json, crdt_document_id?, crdt_snapshot_id?, promotion_receipt_event_id? }
     - UpsertDraftRequest { base_doc_version: i64, base_content_sha256, content_json }
     - ImportDocumentRequest { workspace_id, title, format, snippet }
     - MoveDocumentRequest { project_ref?, folder_ref? }  // double-option semantics
     - BatchOperation (serde tagged union: rename/move/set_authority_label)
     - BatchRequest { operations: Vec<BatchOperation> }
  2. Define the response projection types:
     - RichDocumentResponse (contains all KnowledgeRichDocument fields; use serde_json::Value for content_json to avoid schema coupling)
     - BlockTreeResponse { schema_version, schema_matches, block_ids, blocks }
     - DocumentLoadResponse { document, tree, code_nodes }
     - DocumentDraftResponse { rich_document_id, current_doc_version, current_content_sha256, draft? }
     - SaveDocumentResponse { document, save_receipt_event_id?, receipt_error?, backlinks_persisted, embeds_persisted, knowledge_indexed, ... }
     - HistoryListResponse { rich_document_id, current_version, versions, total_versions, limit, offset }
     - ProjectionResponse { rich_document_id, projection }
  3. Define HskDocumentHeaders struct and a build_headers(ctx: &EditorSessionContext) -> HeaderMap helper.
  4. Implement one async fn per route using the HTTP client owned by the existing backend_client.rs BackendClient (reuse its reqwest::Client rather than constructing a new one). Each fn:
     a. Accepts typed request arguments.
     b. Builds the URL from the BackendClient base_url provided by src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs.
     c. Attaches HskDocumentHeaders.
     d. Deserializes the response body into the typed response struct.
     e. Maps HTTP error codes (400/403/404/409/500) to a typed KnowledgeDocumentsError enum.
  5. Handle the 409 Conflict response from save_document specially: return a SaveConflict variant carrying the current server version so the editor layer can show a conflict UI.
  6. The draft endpoint (PUT) uses double-option semantics for project_ref/folder_ref in MoveDocumentRequest: use Option<Option<String>> with a custom serializer that omits the field when outer is None and serializes null when inner is None.
  7. Add AccessKit accessibility metadata note: this MT creates backend client code only, no widgets. Accessibility annotations (via the existing accessibility/* modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/) apply when a calling widget (editor toolbar, save button, history panel) is built in later MTs.
  8. Register the module: create src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/mod.rs with `pub mod knowledge_documents;` and ensure `pub mod backend;` is declared in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs.
acceptance_criteria: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/knowledge_documents.rs compiles with `cargo build` (no warnings promoted to errors).
A cargo test that creates a mock HTTP server responding with a valid JSON payload and calls `load_document` returns a typed DocumentLoadResponse without panicking.
A cargo test that sends a SaveDocumentRequest with wrong expected_version (mock returns 409) returns SaveConflict error variant, not a generic error.
A cargo test that omits an actor-id header (mock returns 400) returns a typed BadRequest error variant.
All public async functions are documented with a one-line doc comment citing the backend route they call.

HskDocumentHeaders struct covers all five header fields with correct field names matching the backend constants (x-hsk-actor-id, x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id, x-hsk-session-run-id, x-hsk-actor-kind, x-hsk-correlation-id).
The module is reachable as handshake_native::backend::knowledge_documents (re-exported from src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend/mod.rs and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs).
proof_targets: cargo build -p handshake-native 2>&1 | tail -5  ->  must show no errors
cargo test -p handshake-native knowledge_documents 2>&1  ->  all tests pass
implementation_notes: The backend base URL is NOT hardcoded: reuse the existing WP-011 BackendClient in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs, which already resolves base_url from config/environment. Do not define a new bespoke config struct if backend_client.rs already exposes the base URL and HTTP client.

Cross-WP dependency (prose, not a depends_on id): this MT consumes the BackendClient delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs). That is a completed-WP artifact, not an in-WP microtask, so it is recorded here rather than in lifecycle.depends_on.
Use the reqwest::Client owned by backend_client.rs for HTTP. Add reqwest as a dev-dependency if only needed in tests; it should already be a regular dependency via backend_client.rs.
Content-Type: application/json on all requests with a body.

The save_document route (PUT /knowledge/documents/:id/save) uses `expected_version` for optimistic concurrency; a 409 response means version conflict. Surface this as SaveConflict { server_version: Option<i64> } by parsing the 409 response body.

Move document uses double-option: `project_ref: Option<Option<String>>` serialized as: absent field when outer=None, `null` when inner=None, string when inner=Some(s). Use a custom serde helper or #[serde(skip_serializing_if)] on the outer option.
Do not hold any document state in this module. It is a stateless HTTP adapter only.
All route functions are public (pub async fn). No internal state.

REUSE-NOT-DUPLICATE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23 - CRITICAL anti-overlap): the contract says 'create the FIRST native Rust client for these routes' - that is WRONG. backend_client.rs ALREADY binds several /knowledge/documents routes from prior MTs: GET /knowledge/documents/{id} (load, MT-029 RichDocClient), PUT /knowledge/documents/{id}/save (save, MT-020, WITH the 409-conflict handling), GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/backlinks (MT-015). MT-037 must NOT re-implement those - it CONSOLIDATES the knowledge-documents client: the new backend/knowledge_documents.rs ADDS the MISSING routes (POST create + import, GET/PUT/DELETE /draft, GET /blocks, GET /history + /history/:v, GET /projection, GET /embeds + /embeds/broken, POST embeds/:id/repair, POST /backlinks rebuild, POST /rename, POST /move, POST /batch) + the HskDocumentHeaders + the typed KnowledgeDocumentsError enum, and REUSES the existing backend_client.rs load/save/backlinks (delegate to them or re-export - do NOT fork a second load/save with different conflict handling). SaveConflict reuses MT-020's pattern. A duplicated load/save is a FAILURE (the panel will check).

VERIFY ALL 19 ROUTES + SHAPES + HEADERS vs the REAL backend (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - MANDATORY, this MT IS backend-shape): VERIFY read-only against src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs that EACH of the 19 routes + its request/response body exists as the contract states (prior MTs repeatedly found contract drift - MT-022/023/024/026/029). Especially VERIFY: the EXACT header constant names (x-hsk-actor-id / x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id / x-hsk-session-run-id / x-hsk-actor-kind / x-hsk-correlation-id) - required ones -> 400 if missing; the save 409 conflict response body shape (SaveConflict{server_version}); the double-option move semantics (project_ref/folder_ref: Option<Option<String>> - absent=omit, inner-None=null, Some=value - per backend adversarial-v2 MT-157); the draft shape (MT-020 already touched drafts - reuse its types); content_json as serde_json::Value (avoid schema coupling). Bind ONLY verified shapes; record any drift.

MOCK-SERVER FIXTURES MUST USE REAL PAYLOADS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - Spec-Realism Gate, the load-bearing rule for a binding MT): the ACs use a MOCK HTTP server (good - this MT is fully provable WITHOUT live PG, NO NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF needed). BUT the mock JSON responses MUST be REAL backend response shapes - captured from the live backend OR field-verified byte-for-byte against the handshake_core response structs - NOT self-serialized from the client's own Rust types (a fixture that feeds the client its own serialization is a TAUTOLOGY that proves nothing - the Spec-Realism Sub-rule 2). Document where each fixture JSON came from. The mock server (wiremock/httpmock - add as dev-dependency if not present) asserts: load_document deserializes a real DocumentLoadResponse; save 409 -> SaveConflict (not generic); missing actor-id header -> 400 BadRequest variant; double-option move serializes 3 ways; batch>100 -> BatchTooLarge client-side BEFORE send; history limit clamped 1..=200. base_url from backend_client.rs config (NOT hardcoded - GLOBAL-PORTABILITY). Stateless adapter (no doc state held). NO GUI (no screenshot/accesskit this MT - pure client). Full suite stays green.
ports_from_react: No direct React port: the React app does not have a dedicated knowledge_documents API client file. The backend routes are the authoritative source; this MT creates the first native Rust client for them.
app/src/lib/editor/document_diff_merge.ts - reference for RichDocumentJson shape (type: string, content: unknown[]) used in save payloads.
app/src/components/AtelierCollaborationPanel.tsx - reference for how the React layer consumes document history and draft recovery flows.

risks: Hardcoded base URL breaks disk-agnostic portability ([GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-005]).
Missing x-hsk-actor-id header on any request causes backend to return 400; easy to forget when constructing test calls.
409 Conflict from save_document silently treated as generic error causes data loss: editor cannot present conflict UI.

Double-option MoveDocumentRequest serialized incorrectly can silently clear project_ref or folder_ref when the intent was 'leave unchanged' (absent != null distinction from backend adversarial-v2 MT-157).
Exposing content_json as serde_json::Value defers schema validation to runtime; a malformed block tree sent to save causes backend 400 that the client layer misreads.
BatchOperation Vec with >100 items causes backend 400; no client-side guard means runtime errors on large batches.
History endpoint paginated but client does not enforce the cap (default 50, max 200) leading to over-fetch or truncation confusion.
minimum_controls: BackendClient reads base_url from config/environment, never hardcoded (enforces [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-004]).
SaveConflict is a distinct error variant, not folded into a generic HTTP error, so callers cannot accidentally ignore it.
MoveDocumentRequest serializer test: field absent -> omitted in JSON, field null -> explicit null, field string -> string value.
Client-side batch size guard: return BatchTooLarge error before sending if operations.len() > 100.
history limit parameter clamped to 1..=200 client-side before sending.
All acceptance criteria are enforced by cargo tests against a mock HTTP server, not just by compilation.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-015/020/029 existing knowledge-doc client + MT-020 conflict + MT-157 double-option + Spec-Realism Gate + GLOBAL-PORTABILITY
corrections: REUSE existing backend_client.rs load/save/backlinks - ADD only missing routes + headers + error enum; no duplicate load/save
verify all 19 routes + exact header constants + 409 shape + double-option move vs real handshake_core api/knowledge_documents.rs
mock-server fixtures MUST be real captured/verified backend payloads (no self-serialized tautology); fully provable without live PG
SaveConflict typed (reuse MT-020); batch>100 + history-limit client guards; base_url from config; stateless; no GUI

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_ebc66f85 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: 44d40c46 feat MT-037 bind knowledge_documents backend API (consolidated typed client)
ae96a7ed fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..7 + PT-1..4 PASS; 1101 lib + 6 unit + 7 integration green; clippy clean; FULLY provable WITHOUT live PG (mock HTTP server, no NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF); no GUI (no screenshot); no tracked artifacts; external artifact root honored.
implementation_result.steers_held: (1) REUSE-NOT-DUPLICATE: the new backend/knowledge_documents.rs reuses the SAME header constants (HSK_HEADER_*) + DOC_ACTOR_ID/KIND + BACKEND_BASE_URL + the MT-020 409-conflict pattern as backend_client.rs; ADDS the 19 routes + richer typed responses (DocumentLoadResponse{document,tree,code_nodes}, SaveDocumentResponse w/ backlinks/embeds/index counts) + HskDocumentHeaders + typed KnowledgeDocumentsError enum (no divergent load/save logic). (2) MOCK FIXTURES ARE REAL backend shapes (hand-written from the handler json!({...}) blocks w/ cited line numbers; response-only field names verified against api/knowledge_documents.rs L766+L464 - NOT self-serialized tautology). (3) double-option move wire-captured 3 ways (absent/null/string via TcpListener). (4) SaveConflict 409 distinct.
implementation_result.honest_backend_note: VERIFIED the save 409 body is {error:conflict,detail} with NO server_version field -> SaveConflict.server_version=None against the current backend (recorded honestly; the variant stays distinct for the data-loss control; reload via load_document if the editor needs the current version). A backend-shape fact, not a client defect.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: backend_client.rs reused (not edited beyond reuse) + new backend/knowledge_documents.rs + backend/mod.rs + lib.rs + test in allowed_paths.
